The Embedded Component

The component is located in the comp/reuse subfolder. It is kept simple and only contains a Main view that renders a simple button in the Main.view.xml file. It does not declare any models or use any OData services, but this would be possible in the same way as for an application.

The manifest.json defines type as component and an ID which is constructed including the parent library ID and the relative path inside. This is needed to avoid any issues with module path resolution.
It declares the embeddedBy property with the path to the embedding library. This is an important indicator for the platform that the preload package of the component is part of the library preload package. Without it, there might be an unnecessary request at startup which ends with a 404 Not Found error.

{
    "_version": "1.60.0",
    "sap.app": {
        "id": "btp.samples.cdm.multiple.apps.lib.comp.reuse",
        "type": "component",
        "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
        "title": "{{appTitle}}",
        "description": "{{appDescription}}",
        "applicationVersion": {
            "version": "1.0.0"
        },
        "embeddedBy": "../../"
    }
}

There is no need to specify the sap.cloud node here. This will be inherited from the manifest.json of the lib.

The UI5 Apps

For the apps (app1 and app2) there are only minor changes.
In the manifest.json of both apps the dependency to the library has been added.
Additionally there is a component usage for the reuse component.

"sap.ui5": {
    "dependencies": {
        "minUI5Version": "1.95.0",
        "libs": {
            "sap.ui.core": {},
            "sap.m": {},
            "sap.f": {},
            "btp.samples.cdm.multiple.apps.lib": {}
        }
    },
    "componentUsages": {
        "reuse": {
            "name": "btp.samples.cdm.multiple.apps.lib.comp.reuse",
            "settings": {},
            "componentData": {},
            "lazy": false
        }
    }
}

There are no resourceRoots URLs defined here because these depend on the way the apps are started.

The Master.view.xml uses the library by adding the reuse control.
Additionally, there is a component container that shows the component usage.

<mvc:View
	controllerName="btp.samples.cdm.multiple.apps.app1.controller.Master"
	xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
	xmlns:semantic="sap.f.semantic"
	xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns:lib="btp.samples.cdm.multiple.apps.lib">

            <!-- Reuse control from shared lib -->
            <lib:controls.Reuse text="App1" />
            <core:ComponentContainer usage="reuse" manifest="true" async="true" />

</mvc:View>

Routings

The xs-app.json files of the apps have not changed. There is no route for the library added because the library will be accessed directly and not via the apps.

However the ui5.yaml files that define the server for the local run have been enhanced.
They define the entire isolated test environment for an app and therefore also need a route mapping for the static sources of the lib.
The lib is mounted under a subfolder which has the pattern <Service Name (without dots)>.<sap.app/id (without dots)>. The reason the path is named this way will be explained later.

server:
  customMiddleware:
  - name: fiori-tools-servestatic
    afterMiddleware: compression
    configuration:
      paths:
        - path: /btpsamplescdmmultipleapps.btpsamplescdmmultipleappslib
          src: "../lib/src"

Resource Mappings

The index.html needs to define a resource mapping for the library to tell UI5 where to find the resources. It maps the library name to the subfolder defined in the custom middleware.

<script id="sap-ui-bootstrap"
    src="https://ui5.sap.com/resources/sap-ui-cachebuster/sap-ui-core.js"
    data-sap-ui-theme="sap_fiori_3"
    data-sap-ui-resourceroots='{
        "btp.samples.cdm.multiple.apps.app1": "./",
        "btp.samples.cdm.multiple.apps.lib": "./btpsamplescdmmultipleapps.btpsamplescdmmultipleappslib"
    }'
    data-sap-ui-libraries="sap.m"
    data-sap-ui-oninit="module:sap/ui/core/ComponentSupport"
    data-sap-ui-compatVersion="edge"
    data-sap-ui-async="true"
    data-sap-ui-preload="async"
    data-sap-ui-frameOptions="trusted">
</script>

Running locally

Again, to start the local test just run the start or start-local script in one of the package.json files.

The UI5 runtime will be able to load the library using the mapped paths which will be resolved by the local middleware and served from the source folder of the library.

 

Running the Deployed Apps

Deploying the App

Build and deploy the single MTA project in the same way as before.

Running Standalone

After the deployment, the apps can be run standalone from their index.html via the managed approuter. You can find them in the SAP BTP cockpit on the HTML5 Applications page under your subaccount.

Since the same index.html page is used, the same resource mapping for the library will be used, which loads the resources from a sub path with the name <Service Name (without dots)>.<sap.app/id (without dots)>. This makes use of a special feature of the managed approuter, which allows to address dependencies to other HTML5 applications with a relative path. For running locally, the same path has been chosen to configure the custom middleware. This simulates the behavior of the managed approuter and allows you to use the same path and reuse of the index.html.
Formerly this relative path resolution was not supported, and this path could not be hardcoded. Instead, it required a script in the index.html to calculate it. This has been simplified now.

Running in the SAP Build Work Zone Service

To run the in the Launchpad configure it as content provider as described in Part 1 - Running in the SAP Build Work Zone Service. Afterwards you can run the new apps in the same way as before.
Here the platform will provide the URLs to access the apps and the library.

If you check the ui5AppInfo.json that is used to start the app you can find the additional dependencies for the library. The reuse component doesn't appear here, because it is embedded in the library and all paths can be derived from it.
Note that here the paths for the library are not relative to the consuming app but are directly below the root. Both paths are possible and access the same content. It depends on the use case which way is more convenient to use.

{
    "name": "btp.samples.cdm.multiple.apps.app1",
    "url": "/461b5f6c-c16b-4344-bb67-cd5bd3acd019.btpsamplescdmmultipleapps.btpsamplescdmmultipleappsapp1/~a48cdea1-913e-451f-9dee-146e2897b567~/",
    "manifest": true,
    "asyncHints": {
        "libs": [
            {
                "name": "sap.m",
                "lazy": false
            },
            {
                "name": "sap.f",
                "lazy": false
            },
            {
                "name": "btp.samples.cdm.multiple.apps.lib",
                "url": {
                    "url": "/461b5f6c-c16b-4344-bb67-cd5bd3acd019.btpsamplescdmmultipleapps.btpsamplescdmmultipleappslib/~a48cdea1-913e-451f-9dee-146e2897b567~/",
                    "final": true
                },
                "lazy": false
            },
            {
                "name": "sap.ui.fl",
                "lazy": false
            }
        ],
        "components": []
    },
    "messages": [],
    "version": "1.0.0"
}

Next Steps

>You are now ready to build multiple apps and move their common parts into a reusable library.
However, everything is still contained in the same MTA project. In bigger projects, you might need to split this into smaller parts in order to achieve independent lifecycles and split responsibilities.
Watch out for Part 3 of this blog which will be published soon.

entry.title: Learn how to transition from SAP Enterprise Portal to SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
As our business grows and adapts, it’s essential that the platforms we use to connect, collaborate, and enhance productivity keep pace. 

 

SAP Enterprise Portal is the web front-end component for the SAP NetWeaver platform. Thousands of customers around the world are still using SAP Enterprise Portal to provide their employees, customers, and partners a central and intuitive access point to business applications, information, and services. If you are one of those customers, then you should consider the following dates: SAP NetWeaver 7.5 will be supported in mainstream maintenance until end of 2027 with extended maintenance until end of 2030. 

For more information, see SAP NetWeaver 7.5 Maintenance Strategy.  

 

As a general strategic approach of how to proceed as an EP customer, we recommend to transition your SAP Enterprise Portal use cases to SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), multi-cloud environment and in particular to SAP Build Work Zone  We're thrilled to announce our transition from SAP Enterprise Portal to SAP Build Work Zone, an upgraded digital experience offering greater functionality, streamlined workflows, and a more intuitive interface tailored for today’s dynamic workplace. 

 

In this post, we’ll outline what this transition entails, highlight the major enhancements, and provide guidance on maximizing the potential of SAP Build Work Zone. 

 

Why Transition to SAP Build Work Zone? 

SAP Build Work Zone offers significant improvements over SAP Enterprise Portal, which has served us well but now limits our evolving needs. SAP Build Work Zone seamlessly integrates with the latest SAP solutions, providing powerful productivity tools, embedded collaboration features, and simplified customization options. Key benefits include: 

  • Enhanced Collaboration: SAP Build Work Zone integrates collaborative tools directly into workflows, making it easier to connect with colleagues and share information effortlessly. 
  • Personalized Experience: SAP Build Work Zone allows users to personalize their dashboards, making it simple to access the most relevant content and tools. 
  • Unified Access to Applications: With seamless integration to SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, and other cloud solutions, Work Zone provides a single access point to essential tools and data. 

 

Key Differences Between SAP Enterprise Portal and SAP Build Work Zone 

Feature 

SAP Enterprise Portal 

SAP Build Work Zone 

Collaboration 

Limited 

Robust collaboration tools, including SAP Jam and Teams integration 

Customization 

Complex, limited options 

Highly customizable, intuitive for users and admins 

Integration with SAP S/4HANA 

Basic 

Deep, native integration 

User Experience 

Traditional portal-based 

Modern, mobile-optimized, and user-centric 

Cloud Readiness 

Mostly on-premise 

Fully cloud-based solution 

 

 

Getting Started with SAP Build Work Zone 

We’ve aimed for a smooth transition to SAP Build Work Zone. Here’s a quick start guide to help you get up and running: 

  1. Accessing SAP Build Work Zone 
    SAP Build Work Zone is accessible through a new URL, but your login credentials will remain the same. Simply navigate to the new link and sign in with your existing credentials. 
  2. Customizing Your Page 
    On your first login, you’ll have the option to personalize your page. Add widgets to quickly access essential applications, notifications, and favorite content. 
  3. Exploring Integrated Tools 
    SAP Build Work Zone connects seamlessly with collaboration platforms like Microsoft Teams and Sharepoint, along with productivity tools in the SAP suite. Take some time to explore these integrations to help streamline your daily workflows. 
  4. Navigating Workspaces and Groups 
    Workspaces in SAP Build Work Zone replace certain functions previously available in Enterprise Portal pages. Within Workspaces, you can create content, engage in discussions, and share documents with your team. We encourage you to join your department’s workspace and explore available resources. 
  5. Using Knowledge Bases and Widgets 
    The SAP Build Work Zone knowledge base provides quick access to frequently used documents, guides, and tools. Widgets further personalize your experience by offering instant access to the apps and dashboards most relevant to your role. 

 

We will use this blog as an initial resource, with additional blog posts and knowledge base articles to support you throughout your transition journey. 

Link to the guide: Guide

 

Learn more: 

 SAP Build Work Zone on: sap.com / SAP Community 
Discovery Center missions: standard / advanced edition 
SAP Build Work Zone on SAP PartnerEdge 

 

Leaning Journeys 

Check out the dedicated unit for SAP Build Work Zone in the learning journey: Compose and Automate with SAP Build the No-Code Way. 
Understanding the core capabilities of SAP Build Work Zone in "Designing SAP Build Work Zone". 
For more deep dive technical information, check out "Implement and Administer SAP Build Work Zone". 

 

Join also the SAP Builders group and engage with a community of peers to unleash innovation by building apps, process automations, and creating business sites. 

 Enjoy!!

entry.title: Enabling Access to On-Premise S/4HANA Applications in SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition
entry.content:
Subscriptions

  • SAP Business Technology Platform
  • SAP Cloud Connector
  • SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition
  • S/4 HANA On-Premises System Setup

Pre-requisite Setup

  • Setup of SAP Business Technology Platform Account
  • Setup of SAP Cloud Connector & Trust to the SAP S/4HANA System
  • Setup of S/4HANA on-premises System
  • Setup SAP Identity and Authentication Service (optional)

Pre-requisite Checks

  1. In your BTP subaccount you are subscribed to the following subscriptions
  • Cloud Identity Services

  • SAP Business Application Studio (Automatically Added)
  • SAP Build Work Zone, Standard edition

SAP BTP Subaccount --> Instances and Subscriptions --> create

 

  • Environment – SAP Cloud Foundry Instance

  1. Configure Cloud Connector with S/4 HANA On-Premise System with the following details –

For the installation of the Cloud Connector please follow the steps in the SAP Help document SAP Help

You can skip installing the Cloud Connector if you’re using a demo backend system from SAP Cloud Appliance Library (CAL). These demo systems come with a pre-installed Cloud Connector that’s ready to use. To access it, go to the Welcome page in your CAL system, where you’ll find the relevant links. The Cloud Connector typically runs at http://<IP of your SAP CAL backend>:8443/, where you can directly connect to it.

 

  1. Check your BTP Connectivity with Cloud Connector:- login to your BTP Subaccount and go to Destinations, and select  Create Destination with the following details :

 

 

Field

Value

Name

Name of the Destination

Type

HTTP

URL

http://Virtual Host Port No.(443XX)

Proxy Type

OnPremise

Authentication

BasicAuthentication

User

Support User Created in On-Premise System

 

 

           

 

  1. Once the Connection is successful, test the mapping created in Cloud Connector, it should be reachable.

 

 

Execution Steps

 

Create a Site in SAP Build Work Zone

In SAP Build Work Zone, a "site" refers to a digital workspace or portal where users can access applications, content, and resources. It is the central hub for delivering a personalized and unified experience to employees, partners, or customers. Sites in SAP Build Work Zone are highly customizable and can be designed to meet specific user needs and business requirements.

  1. Login to SAP BTP Subaccount and go to instances and subscriptions, choose SAP Build Work Zone and Go to Application

In a new window, you will log in to SAP Build Work Zone Site.

Here Select Create Site.

Keep the default settings for the site same and your site is created.

Note – Make sure you have the necessary authorizations assigned to your user ID in SAP BTP. The following roles are mandatory – Launchpad_Admin, Launchpad_External_User

In SAP BTP Subaccount to Security --> Users --> select Assign User Collection

 

Assign these roles to your User ID.

Create Destinations in SAP BTP

To display different types of app interfaces in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition (like on-premise SAP S/4HANA apps and cloud-based apps), you first need to set up a way for Work Zone to access them. This is done by creating HTTP connections, called as "runtime destination, design time destination" in the SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) cockpit. This connection acts as a bridge, allowing Work Zone to securely access and display apps from both your on-premise systems and the cloud.

Creating Design-time Destination

The design-time destination defines the location from which to fetch the content that is exposed by the content provider in SAP Build Work Zone.

  1. In the SAP BTP cockpit, log on to your subaccount as administrator.
  2. Go to Connectivity --> Destinations.
  3. To configure the design-time destination, choose New Destination and enter the following information:

 

Field

Value

Name

Add the dt suffix to the name

Type

HTTP

URL

https://<VIRTUAL_HOST>:<VIRTUAL_PORT>/sap/bc/ui2/cdm3/entities

Proxy Type

OnPremise

Authentication

BasicAuthentication

User

To connect to the SAP S/4HANA system, use a technical user on the destination with permission to read the CDM, for example, the technical user with the SAP_FLP_EXP_USER role, such as <SAP S/4HANA user ID> Creating System User in SAP

 

 

By choosing Additional Properties  New Property -->  sap-client: <client number>

Check the Connection for the Destination.

 

Creating Run-time Destination

The runtime destination defines the location from which to consume the content required to run the federated apps in runtime.

  1. To configure the runtime destination, choose New Destination and enter the following information:

Field

Value

Name

Add the rt suffix to the name

Type

HTTP

URL

https://<VIRTUAL_HOST>:<VIRTUAL_PORT>/sap/bc/ui2/cdm3/entities

Proxy Type

OnPremise

Authentication

BasicAuthentication

User

To connect to the SAP S/4HANA system, use a technical user on the destination with permission to read the CDM, for example, the technical user with the SAP_FLP_EXP_USER role, such as <SAP S/4HANA user ID> Creating System User in SAP

 

Add Following Additional Properties to complete configuration of runtime destination.

Field

Value

HTML5.DynamicDestination

true

sap-client

Client No

sap-platform

ABAP

sap-sysid

On-Premise System ID

sap-service

32XX (XX- virtual Port)

And Check the Connection for this destination it should be successful.

 

Note - The destination name must contain only lowercase letters and numbers. Do not use the underscore (_) character.

Expose Authorization from On-Premise System

This step makes your on-premise SAP roles accessible in SAP Build Work Zone, allowing end users to access integrated Fiori applications directly within the Work Zone site.

  1. Login to SAP On-Premise system and go to T-code - /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE

Add the relevant SAP Roles you want to expose to SAP Build Work Zone.

  1. Click on Expose Content

Make sure the execution is without any errors.

Configure Site in Work Zone

  1. Log in to your SAP Build Work Zone Instance, navigate to Content Channel --> New --> Content Provider

 

  1. Add the Following Details and Save.

Note – Please specify your correct runtime and design time destinations created for build work zone.

  1.  Update the content, here the content provider should get updated without any errors.

In case you receive any errors in connection please verify your destination names , for mapping errors check connectivity with the cloud connector.

Once the content is updated you will have your roles exposed from on premise available in work zone site.

  1. Update the Content From Content Provider

Select your content provider

Add your exposed roles

Roles are now visible in content manager

  1. Go to site Directory --> Settings --> Edit

The exposed roles will be available,

Assign them to the site and Save.

  1. Add the roles in your BTP subaccount

Login to your BTP Subaccount with administrator user and go to security --> Users --> Assign Role Collections

Assign the exposed SAP roles to the user

  1. Now in your SAP Build Work Zone Site the apps are visible

Thank You.

entry.title: Integration architecture guide: Updated version available now
entry.content:
I am happy to share the news that an updated version of the integration architecture guide has been released! Based on SAP Business Technology Platform offerings available today, this document provides you with detailed guidance on how to evolve your existing integration architecture. As before the integration guide is structured along the concepts of the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology, such as integration styles and integration use case patterns.

This blog post gives you an overview about the most important news delivered with this version of the guide.

Process Integration

This chapter has been updated to include recommendations regarding the latest capabilities of SAP Integration Suite such as the new Edge Integration Cell option, Data Space Integration and Advanced Event Mesh.

You can also find consolidated information about how to elevate toward SAP Integration Suite – be it from SAP Process Integration/Orchestration or from SAP API Management and/or SAP Cloud Integration on SAP BTP Neo cloud platform. In the first case, Edge Integration Cell plays an important role: Deployed in customer-controlled environments, it allows you to run your integration scenarios locally within your own environments, without going through the public internet. Migrated existing integrations flows can be redeployed to and run by the Edge Integration Cell.

 

Scenarios using Edge Integration Cell

Event-Driven Integration

Event-driven architecture (EDA) is a well-known approach for designing and building decoupled, hyper-connected software systems and it’s rap­idly gaining popularity in the world of enterprise integration due to its reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness. This chapter describes the role and interplay of SAP EDA solutions such as SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, SAP Cloud Application Event Hub, further capabilities of SAP Integration Suite and technologies for event-enabling SAP applications.

Event-Driven Architecture in the Enterprise

Data Integration

The guide also describes the role of SAP Datasphere with regards to data integration options. SAP Datasphere enables a business data fabric architecture and supports integration use case pattern like data ingestion, virtual data access, and data transformation and orchestration.

Overview of SAP Datasphere

User Integration

There is also a major overhaul of this chapter to provide recommendations regarding the latest SAP solutions which enable a central entry point for users to business solutions. These are SAP Start, SAP Mobile Start, SAP Build Work Zone (standard and advanced edition) and SAP Task Center.

Cross-solutions entry point on SAP Business Technology Platform

Furthermore, the technology recommendation for the chatbot integration use case pattern of the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology reflects now the latest innovations by introducing Joule: Joule is a generative AI-based assistant embedded in SAP’s cloud enterprise portfolio that provides proactive, contextualized insights from various data sources.

New Content Format and Access Point

In addition to updating the content of the integration architecture guide we also changed its format: The guide is now available as documentation at SAP Help Portal and comes with benefits such as easier navigation and search, option to bookmark, subscribe and to provide feedback to the content.

The integration architecture guide is an integral part of SAP BTP Guidance Framework which is the central access point for architects, developers and administrators to build and run enterprise-grade solutions on SAP BTP.

Integration architecture guide at SAP BTP Guidance Framework

The integration guide belongs to the decision guides of the framework and is interconnected with other assets of SAP BTP Guidance Framework such as the documentation of the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology and SAP BTP reference architectures (for integration).

Enjoy reading the new guide! It would also be great to get your feedback, for instance which integration topics should be addressed with the next update. If you have any suggestions feel free to leave them as comments to this blog post. Happy integrating 😊

entry.title: Architecting Email Approval without My Inbox for SAP Build Process Automation Workflows: Part 1
entry.content:
Business Context

While working day in and day out on SAP BTP use cases, we have noticed a growing inclination among customers towards “Automation” specially with SAP BTP SAP Build Process Automation. This trend has led to the development of various side-by-side custom extensions on SAP BTP utilizing SAP Build Process Automation.

Problem Statement

One of the most common requests we receive from multiple customers is for a mechanism that allows email-based approval without the need to manually log into the My Inbox application.

Currently, there is no out-of-the-box capability available to fulfill this requirement. Therefore, we will be developing our own custom module to address this need.

In this three-part blog series, my colleague & BTP Architect @Adwait_Fadnavis  and I will provide insights on how to implement Email-based approval using SAP Build Process Automation capabilities.

We have prepared the content for this series in three blogs, where we will be diving deep into Design, Development, and Testing.

  • Blog 1 (This blog) – This blog will focus on the Business process, context, problem statement, end to end development flow, and required prerequisites.
  • Blog 2 This blog will focus on development and will provide a detailed walkthrough of the development process.
  • Blog 3: This blog will focus on complete end-to-end testing, helping to understand the overall objective.

Scenario Explanation

In this scenario, a workflow will be triggered using SAP Build Process Automation, which will send an email notification to the respective approver.

“The approver will receive the email notification and can reply to this email with either “Approve” or “Reject.” 

Based on the response, the corresponding workflow task will be approved or rejected.

Important Considerations

  1. Valid Approver Verification: The approver must be a valid approver, and the email must come from the approver’s registered email address. If the email does not come from the proper approver, or if the response is not “Approved” or “Reject,” the system must send an email back indicating that the response is not valid.
  2. Unique Request Number: Each approval request must generate a unique request number. This number will be used to validate the response against the original request, ensuring that the approval or rejection is correctly matched to the corresponding workflow task.

Disclaimer:  We can't recall the email after the approval/rejection.

End to End Business Process Flow: Streamlining Approval Processes with SAP BPA Email-Based Automation

End to End Process Flow : Email based Approval

  1. Workflow Initiation

The process begins when an SAP BPA workflow is triggered. This could be due to various business events, such as a purchase order requiring approval.

  1. Sending Approval Work Items : Once triggered, the workflow sends approval work items to the approver’s SAP BTP My Inbox. Simultaneously, an email notification is dispatched to the approver.
  2. Email Notification Details :The email notification includes essential details like the “workflow instance ID” or “Custom Request Number” in the subject line. This ensures the approver & automation (which will elaborate later) can easily identify the request.
  3. Approver’s Response Options: The approver has two options to respond:
  • Log into SAP BTP My Inbox: Approvers can log in and approve or reject the request directly.
  • Email-Based Response: Approvers can reply to the email with “Approve” or “Reject.”

Background Automation

An SAP BPA-based automation runs continuously in the background, monitoring the responses sent by the approver. This automation checks if the response is from a valid approver and corresponds to a valid work item.

  1. Updating Work Items: If the response is valid, the automation updates the SAP BPA work item accordingly. This eliminates the need for the approver to log into My Inbox separately, streamlining the process.
  2. Completion Notification: Upon completion of the process, an approval email is sent to confirm the outcome. This ensures all stakeholders are informed of the decision.

Benefits of Email-Based Approval Automation

  • Efficiency: Reduces the time required for approvals by allowing responses directly via email.
  • Convenience: Approvers can respond from anywhere without needing to log into the system.
  • Accuracy: Automated monitoring ensures only valid responses are processed, reducing errors.

Initial Setup and Configuration for Inbound SMTP-Based Approval

Implementing an email-based approval system using SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) requires a few essential setup steps. Below steps will walk you through the initial configuration to ensure a smooth and efficient process.

Outbound SMTP Destination in SAP Build Process Automation

To send emails from SAP Build Process Automation to users’ email accounts, you need to configure an outbound SMTP destination. This setup is crucial for enabling the system to dispatch approval email notifications.

Inbound SMTP Email Configuration

For receiving email responses from approvers, we will use Google for both inbound and outbound SMTP. This setup can also be adapted for Microsoft email or other email services. Here are the key steps:

  1. Enable Access for Reading Gmail: Ensure that the Gmail account used for receiving responses has the necessary permissions enabled. For detailed instructions, refer to the relevant  sap documentation.

Save Credentials JSON File: After setting up the Google Cloud Platform(GCP), securely save the credentials. These credentials are necessary for the SAP BPA to access and read incoming emails.External User Setup in SAP BPA Control Tower: Complete the configuration for the external user in the SAP BPA Control Tower. This step ensures that the system can correctly route and process incoming emails from approvers.

SAP BTP BPA Control Tower

 

Setting Up External Authentication for the Google(Gmail)

Desktop Agent Installation and External User configuration (SMTP Inbound)

The Desktop Agent must be installed in unattended mode to run background processes efficiently.

It is recommended to install the Desktop Agent on a virtual machine to ensure continuous operation. Follow these steps:

Desktop Agent Sub Account Setup: Configure the Desktop Agent within the appropriate sub-account.

  1. Set to Unattended Mode: For real-time production scenarios, set the Desktop Agent to unattended mode. This setup is crucial for automating the approval process without manual intervention.
  2. Configure External User: Ensure that the external user configured in the previous step is correctly set up in the Desktop Agent. This configuration allows the agent to process email responses and update the SAP BPA work items accordingly.

Desktop Agent - Registering External Authentication

By following these steps, you are all set for robust email-based approval system using SAP BPA, streamlining your approval processes and enhancing efficiency.

Design and Development Walkthrough for SMTP Inbound Email-Based Approval Automation 

Once the initial setup and configuration are complete in the SAP BPA Control Tower and the unattended Desktop Agent is installed & configured, the next step is to design and develop the automation components.

Below is a summary of the key components involved in this process. My colleague @Adwait_Fadnavis  will provide a detailed walkthrough in a subsequent blog post.

The first step is to create a BPA automation. This involves several key actions:

  1. Invoke Google Authentication and Search for New Emails:

    • Authenticate External Gmail: Set up Google authentication to allow the system to access your Gmail account.
    • Search Gmail: Create a step to search for new, unread emails with the appropriate subject and request ID. Ensure that only unread emails are read to avoid processing duplicates.
    • Validate the Approver:

    • Check Valid Approver Validation : Validate that the response is from an authorized approver by reading the subject line for the request number.
    • Fetch Work Item Details: Use the SAP BPA API to retrieve work item details and match the approver. If the approver is not valid or authorized, send an error message.
    • Read the Email Body and Response:

    • Process Response: If the email response contains “Approve” or “OK,” call the SAP BPA API to update the workflow task accordingly.
    • Send Completion Email: Notify Stakeholders: After successfully processing the workflow approval or rejection, send an email notification to inform all relevant parties of the outcome.

In the upcoming blog post, @Adwait_Fadnavis  will delve into the development flow in detail and provide valuable insights on how to effectively develop inbound SMTP. Final Development flow chart will look as below:

End to End: Development FlowStay tuned for a comprehensive guide that will enhance your understanding and streamline your Development  process.

Regards,

Mohit Bansal, SAP BTP & S4HANA Architect

entry.title: Architecting Email Approval without My Inbox for SAP Build Process Automation Workflows: Part 3
entry.content:
This is the third and final blog of the series, in which we would like to explain the testing part in detail.

We have kept testing separate in this blog to provide a comprehensive explanation of all scenarios and steps from a technical perspective.

We have prepared the content for this series in three blogs, where we will be diving deep into Design, Development, and Testing.

  • Blog 1 – This blog will focus on the Business process, context, problem statement, end to end development flow, and required prerequisites.
  • Blog 2 This blog will focus on development and will provide a detailed walkthrough of the development process.
  • Blog 3(This blog) : This blog will focus on complete end-to-end testing, helping to understand the overall objective.

In the previous blog, @Adwait_Fadnavis  walked you through the complete development process. Now, I would like to start with the testing part.

Detailed Example of the Testing Process

To illustrate the testing process in more detail, let’s walk through an example:

  1. Automation Scheduling: The automation is scheduled to run every 15 minutes. The desktop agent is set up on a virtual machine to ensure it runs in unattended mode.
  2. Dummy Workflow Setup: A dummy workflow is created with fields such as “Request ID,” “Requester Name,” and “Approval Status.” Upon submission, an email is sent to the approver.
  3. Email Response Handling: The approver receives the email and responds with “Approve.” The automation script checks the email subject line and sender’s email address to validate the response.
    1. Invalid Response / Invalid Approver Handling: If the response is not valid or from an unauthorized approver, the system logs the incident and notifies the relevant parties for further investigation.
    2. Approver Handling: If the response is valid or from an authorized approver,  Workflow task will be approved.
    3. Rejection Scenario: If the approver responds with “Reject,” the workflow status is updated to “Rejected,” and an email notification is sent to the requester and other stakeholders.
  4. Workflow Update: The workflow status is updated to “Approved,” and an email notification is sent to the requester and other stakeholders.
  5. Sending Notification Emails: Email Notification to all stake holders involved.

Let's Deep dive above steps in Detail:

Step 1: Automation Scheduled as Background Job

In a real-time scenario, this automation can be scheduled as a background job at intervals of 15 minutes, 30 minutes, or any custom-defined time interval.

The frequency can be adjusted based on the volume of approval tasks. This scheduling ensures that the system checks for new approval requests regularly and processes them in a timely manner.

As mentioned in the first blog, it is mandatory to have the desktop agent running in unattended mode. This setup is crucial for ensuring that the automation runs smoothly without manual intervention.

For real-time business scenarios, we recommend installing the desktop agent on virtual machines to ensure continuous operation and to handle high volumes of approval tasks efficiently.

Job scheduling : SAP Build Process Automation

Step 2: (Optional) Triggering the Workflow -  Prepare the Dummy Workflow for Quick Testing

To facilitate quick testing, we prepared a small workflow using SAP Build Process Automation with a few fields. This dummy workflow simulates the real-time process and helps in validating the automation setup. Although in real-time, the workflow will be triggered via a valid application, here is a snapshot of our dummy workflow for demonstration purposes:

  • Workflow Trigger: Upon clicking the submit button, the workflow is triggered.
  • Email Notification: An email is sent to the approver for approval. This step involves configuring an SMTP outbound email, as mentioned in the previous blog post.

This setup allows us to test the end-to-end process in a controlled environment before deploying it in a live scenarioriggering the Workflow -  Prepare the Dummy Workflow for Quick Testing (Optional)

Test for Sending Email for Task Creation

Sample Email Body

Triggering Workflow : Testing Demonstrations:

Process Triggered and Sent for Approval

 Step 3: Handling Email Responses

Upon receiving the email, the approver will have three options: a) Approve b) Reject c) Not a valid response

Let’s say the approver starts with “ok” and responds with this in the email.

Upon receiving the response, our automation running as a background job will check if the response is coming from a valid approver and if the subject line is correct (the subject line should not be modified).

This validation step is crucial to ensure that only authorized responses are processed.

Validating Correct ApproverValidating Correct Approvers Response will be send if the Approver is not valid.

Response will be send if the Approver is not valid.

Validating Correct response: Validating Correct response

Step 4: Workflow Update on Approval

If the work item is approved, the workflow will move forward and update the task accordingly. This involves:

  • Updating the Workflow Status: The status of the workflow is updated to “Approved” in the system

Workflow Update on Approval

  • Notifying Stakeholders: All relevant stakeholders are notified about the approval via email.

Notifying Stakeholders

Workflow Update on Rejection

Similarly, if the response is “no” or “Rejected,” the work item will be updated, and the workflow will proceed with the rejected branch. This involves:

  • Updating the Workflow Status: The status of the workflow is updated to “Rejected” in the system.

Updating the Workflow Rejection Status

  • Notifying Stakeholders: All relevant stakeholders are notified about the rejection via email.

Step 5: Sending Notification Emails

An email notification will be sent to all involved parties to inform them of the status of the workflow request. This step ensures transparency and keeps everyone informed about the progress of the approval process.

rejection via email

We hope you enjoyed reading our blog series by me and @Adwait_Fadnavis  on Architecting Email Approval for SAP Build Process Automation Workflows. We trust that this approach provides valuable insights into managing workflow approvals efficiently without relying on manual My Inbox / Task Center approvals.

Thank you for following along with us.

Regards,

Mohit Bansal l, SAP BTP & S/4HANA Architect , Linkedin

entry.title: SAP Build 中的新增功能 – 2024 年第 3 季度版本
entry.content:
本文为翻译文，原文链接 What’s New in SAP Build – Q3 2024 Edition, 原作者 EsmeeX。

你好，开发者！ 我们很高兴为您介绍 SAP Build 第 3 季度的新增功能系列。 在此版本中，我们将介绍针对以下内容的产品特定更新：

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Work Zone、SAP Start、SAP Task Center
  • SAP Build Code

SAP Build Apps

版本管理和项目生命周期管理：现在可以使用 SAP Cloud Transport Management 系统跨不同租户发布和传输项目。在隔离 SAP Build Apps 应用程序到其自有架构的开发、测试和生产部署时，您可以在子账户之间传输项目版本。要开始，请参阅此博客帖子。

包含 S/4HANA 中大型数据集的性能表列表：管理和显示应用程序中的大量数据可能具有挑战性并导致性能问题。查看此示例了解如何为具有 65k+ 记录的 S/4HANA Cloud 端点创建性能列表页面 - 请参阅博客帖子。

在一个项目中复制页面：此功能使用户能够在 SAP Build Apps 的项目中复制和复制页面，这是一个简单但功能强大的扩展组件，可进一步加快开发速度。

新的构建服务：我们最近推出了全新的改进体验来部署应用程序包。构建服务包括管理图像资产、iOS 使用字符串、移动服务启用、打包创建和配置管理。更新的 UI 在部署阶段为开发人员提供了更顺畅的体验，确保满足每个要求并且您的应用已准备好发布。试试吧！

  • 另请查看路多·诺恩斯 (Ludo Noens) 系列视频，了解如何借助 SAP Build Apps 使用 SAP 解决方案，创建 CRUD 应用
  • 详细了解 SAP Build Apps 路线图的最新动态。

SAP Build Process Automation

SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud：SAP S/4HANA 公共云的 2408 版现在提供可扩展性向导。这使得并排扩展的创建更加简化，并允许您从cloud ERP 无缝导航到 SAP Build。第一个版本支持自定义流程创建，SAP Build 今年将提供更多选项。有关详细信息，请参阅博客。

Script Task Editor (脚本任务编辑器)：现在可以在新的脚本编辑器中编写 JavaScript 代码，从而允许在流程上下文中执行复杂的数据类型转换和附加操作。之前，这仅限于自动化 - 现在包括流程输入和自定义变量，有助于高级数据操作和转换。在博客帖子中查看更多信息。

计划触发器：现在，您可以在运行时根据用户定义的时间间隔和日期计划流程自动化。这允许您在创建自动化方案时启用以系统为中心和基于时间的流程自动化用例，例如工作日和非工作日，以避免资源不匹配。

表单中的附加结果和 UI5 任务支持：这使开发人员能够在流程中配置附加结果操作，以便在审批表单中缺少信息或信息错误时让审批人请求返工。您还可以通过引导式体验有效地开始填写多个表单。

通过手动说明上传创建高级事件项目：这允许用户上传业务事件规范，以在大厅中创建事件项目。新功能增加了对跨业务范围 (LOB) 和自定义事件的业务事件的支持，包括在运行时使用 SAP Event Mesh 支持的第三方事件。

流程可见性增强：包括切实可行的洞察、针对请求者的实例跟踪以及通过 CALM 提供的附加监控功能。现在，您可以将洞察转化为有意义的结果，并根据可见性洞察触发上报流，以直接响应和解决关键情况。这样可实现可审计和治理的上报解决方案，而不是在不同渠道断开通信。

了解有关 SAP Build Process Automation 的更多信息：

  • 有关更多更新和改进，请参阅 What's New Viewer（新增功能查看器）。
  • 有关更多即将推出的功能和重点区域，请参阅 SAP Road Map Explorer。

SAP Build Work Zone、SAP Start 和 SAP Task Center

SAP Build Work Zone（两个版本）

HTML5 业务解决方案作为内容提供者：通过 SAP Build Work Zone 中的最新更新，您现在可以以“通用数据模型”(CDM) 格式将基于 BTP 的应用程序公开为 Work Zone 内容，例如角色、空间和页面。因此，您可以轻松配置和启用从 Work Zone 实例访问 BTP 应用程序的功能，甚至可以跨子账户访问。 请参阅帮助门户和此博客帖子中的更多信息。

SAP Build Work Zone（标准版）

定义页面和空间的排列顺序：管理员现在可以配置在 SAP Build Work Zone（标准版）的菜单栏上对空间和页面进行排序。仅当在空间和页面的新体验查看模式(new experience view mode)下工作时，此功能才适用。请参阅帮助门户中的更多信息。

页面和空间的导入和导出：将增强内容管理器工具，使管理员能够在架构之间导出和导入自定义（本地创建的）空间和页面。

SAP Build Work Zone（高级版）

跨 SAP Build Work Zone 和 Process Automation 的集成引导式体验：现在，引导式流程可用作 SAP Build Process Automation 中的部件。除了之前可用的“无模板”向导外，现在还可以在 SAP Build Work Zone 中创建基于流程的引导式向导。请参阅博客帖子中的更多信息。

SAP Start

直接导航到集成云解决方案的产品主页：SAP Start 站点提供了一个新产品分区，可以轻松查看和导航到集成解决方案的产品主页。这使当前用户的可用云解决方案透明化，并快速轻松地导航以启动所需解决方案的产品主页。

SAP Task Center

ITCE 合作伙伴页面：SAP Task Center 现在允许与第三方任务提供者连接。利用此功能的第一个解决方案是由 ITCE 与 SAP 架构师和技术顾问合作开发的 TaskConnect。该应用程序很快将在 SAP Store 中提供，提供全面的配置指南、安装手册和 BPMN 图表。有关 TaskConnect 的详细信息，请参阅此处。

有关 SAP Build Work Zone、SAP Start 和 SAP Task Center 的详细信息：

  • 查看 SAP Build Work Zone 标准版和高级版和 SAP Start/SAP Task Center 的路线图
  • 新增功能查看器：标准版/高级版/SAP Start/SAP Task Center 

SAP Build Code

新增的 AI 功能：在 SAP Build Code 中，您现在可以使用 Joule copilot（我们的生成式 AI 助手）通过提供自然语言提示编写代码。关于Joule的详细解释,请阅读此文。在过去几个月中，我们为此功能添加了许多新功能，包括以下亮点：

  • 支持面向数据模型和应用逻辑的 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 服务集成：现在可以使用Joule为集成的 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 服务生成代码。
  • SAP Build Code 中的 Joule 主要用户体验更新：借助 SAP Build Code 内的 Joule 的最新用户体验更新，我们提高了可用性，简化了工作流，并创建了更直观的界面，提高了生产力和满意度。
  • 生产力工具的生成式AI 支持：我们现在允许您直接从故事板使用Joule。
  • 使用Joule增强现有的数据模型和服务：现在可以使用提示修改现有的数据模型和服务实体。

为什么选择 SAP Build Code：查看产品经理 Rupa Maity 发布的博文，了解为什么迁移至 SAP Build Code。

了解最新的构建代码创新成果：您是否希望随时了解 SAP Build Code 的最新产品更新？在 SAP Community 上关注我们的标签，并订阅 SAP Build Code 简报。 

  • 请参阅 SAP Build Code 自 2024 年 9 月起的最新更新产品更新。
  • 了解有关 SAP Build Code 社区的更多信息。

--- 

 

请注意，此博客帖子提供了最新功能和增强的亮点。 

  • 有关所有更新的完整列表，请参阅 Help Portal SAP Build 中的新增内容。
  • 加入 SAP Builders group，获取产品资源和社区问题。
  • 不要错过 SAP Build-themed sessions 等。
  • 这意味着 SAP TechEd 就在眼前！在 2024 年 TechEd 大会上，深入了解 SAP Build。

您最想测试哪些功能？请在评论中告诉我们！

entry.title: SAP & AGILITA Workshop on SAP Build: what a great experience!
entry.content:
At the end of last week I had a very stimulating trip to Berlin, where I participated in the SAP & AGILITA Workshop on SAP Build. It was an interesting event and I'm happy to share my takeaways with you.

Thursday November 27, 8:30 AM: I join my colleagues who arrived from Walldorf and Helsinki for a breakfast at our hotel, and we quickly head up to SAP Berlin office. Once there, together with the Berlin colleagues, we set up the event rooms we had booked for the keynote and then the hands-on workshop.

At 10.30, attendees start to arrive: we have the pleasure to welcome business users and developers from Berliner Verkehrsbetriebe (BVG), Bundesdruckerei, Stiftung Das Rauhe Haus, Hochschule für Technik und Wirtschaft Berlin as well as partners and a few SAP employees. All together, we engage discussions over a buffet of snacks before the event kicks off at 11.00 with our keynote, joined by many attendees on LinkedIn live and moderated by Esmeé.

Keynote and solution deep dive

Diana Roesner starts the keynote by introducing the Clean Core concept and explaining how SAP Build can be used to easily create side-by-side extensions to an SAP application. On this very important topic, I highly recommend you a new white paper - “Clean core extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud” - and a crisp and easy-to consume learning journey - “Practicing Clean Core Extensibility For SAP S/4HANA Cloud”.

We then dive into the different components of the SAP Build solution, with my close colleagues Kanishka presenting SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Code, and Yumnah describing SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Work Zone. Jesse gives a great demo of how SAP Build can help 🎅🏻 Santa Company 🎄 streamline order approvals by creating an extension directly from his SAP S/4HANA ERP using our Extensibility Wizard. Finally Hemant provides an insightful perspective on how SAP Build supports line-of-businesses, including finance, procurement, supply chain, customer experience, human resources, IT and cross-functional:

Following the presentations, our experts held a Q&A session with the audience, answering the questions raised on-site as well as in the online chat:

We received very positive feedback from the audience on this session, which you can watch as a replay.

You can also download the slides of this keynote.

Hands-on Workshop

After a short break, we moved to another room for the hands-on workshop. In 3 hours, our local participants had the opportunity to create a full-stack application to improve customer loyalty. The purpose of the application is to allow customers to redeem points from their purchases. To do this, the application needs to access customer, purchase and product data from SAP S/4HANA.

Specifically, workshop participants experienced using SAP Build Code to create a back-end CAP service with Joule AI copilot, highlighting SAP's generative AI power for development. The customer loyalty application is connected to SAP S/4HANA Cloud as an extension to the ERP, so the participants learned how to use a BTP destination. And they used SAP Build Apps to create a mobile app, showcasing our modern low-code/no-code development tool.

This is the part of the event I was involved in, and I have to say it was great and rewarding to see our participants amazed at how Joule helped them create data entities, sample data and JavaScript code from simple prompts.  They really experienced the power of Generative AI that we showcased at SAP TechEd a few weeks ago!

If you would like to do this hands-on exercise and experience Joule by yourself, it’s very easy!

Indeed, it is available here in our Basic Trial environment, where you will have immediate access to all the tools needed: SAP Build Code - Generative AI-powered development with Joule copilot.

Christmas Market at AGILITA’s

At 16:00 we jumped into a bus who drove us to the AGILITA office in Berlin. There, AGILITA Christmas Market brought everyone together to reflect and share ideas in a festive atmosphere.

Thomas Neuhaus, Head of Digital Experience for AGILITA, presented SAP AppHaus Workshops: SAP and AGILITA are helping customers find the right path to digitalization with design and architecture thinking workshops. Customers discover how to integrate Al and innovation for their own use cases, and how to effectively improve their business processes.

We also had the opportunity to attend very interesting demos of applications developed by the AGILITA team with SAP Build, showing how tasks like recording time or logging work can happen in just 3 clicks:  

AGIL AI.TimeManager  - Optimize your time and activity recording with AGIL Time Manager, the flexible and user- friendly solution for efficient time management and seamless integration in real-time.
➡️ More on AGIL AI.TimeManager in SAP Store.

 

AGIL Al.Sales  - Boost sales performance with the power of voice and Al synergy. Effortlessly dictate orders and meeting notes while on the go - advanced generative Al transcribes, analyzes, and seamlessly updates your systems in real time.
➡️ More on AGIL AI.Sales in SAP Store.

 Discover more AGILITA’s applications and extensions in SAP Store.

Up to you now!

You too can take advantage of the content we have prepared for this event. And it's very simple!

  1. Watch the keynote replay 🎞  ️
  2. Download the presentation 👍🏻  
  3. Do the hands-on exercise 👩🏻‍🎓👨🏻‍🎓

 

Personal note

To end this blog post on a more personal note, I would like to say that I had 2 very energizing days in Berlin. I only work 3 days a week, Monday-Wednesday, but when I was asked if I could attend this event in Berlin on a Thursday, I didn't hesitate for a second: as I work 100% from home in Lyon, it was a great opportunity to meet up with colleagues from Berlin and Walldorf. The day after the event, we took time to reflect on what we did in 2024 and what we would like to do in 2025. This trip to Berlin was also a nice opportunity to meet great people from our valued partner AGILITA.

And when I was on my way back to Lyon on Friday evening, what caught my eye in the Berlin airport terminal?

On a big screen, an ad from SAP promoting our magical trifecta of Process + Data + AI 😊

entry.title: Joule on Mobile & Start Screen Personalization: SAP Mobile Start V2.1 - Release Update
entry.content:
Just recently, Joule - our generative AI copilot turned 1!
In this one year, Joule has revolutionized the way how users interact with their business solutions, be it HR, Sales, Procurement or IT – but there was one significant piece missing: mobile access.

With our latest releases of SAP Mobile Start, you can now use Joule while being mobile. Instead of searching for the right app, opening it, and then looking for the info, you can now ask Joule in natural language.

While our teams add more and more business content/ scenarios and functions to Joule, we also plan to embed Joule into further mobile apps, like SAP SuccessFactors or SAP Service and Asset Manager.

But now, let´s have a look at how Joule within SAP Mobile Start helps you work faster, get smarter insights when you need them, and interact more smoothly with your business systems while being mobile.

The Joule mobile client is embedded within the SAP Mobile Start header bar and can be opened with a single tap from any main screen. It provides the same main functionality and content as the desktop client if the content is enabled for mobile usage.

Screenshot: SAP Mobile Start with embedded Joule

After opening the Joule client, you just enter your commands in natural language and Joule provides you with the respective answer.

Let´s have a look at three concrete examples:
Imagine you are in a meeting and need to check on your sales orders that have not been delivered so far. With Joule in SAP Mobile Start, you just ask “Show sales orders with delivery status not completed” and Joule will provide you the respective list.

 Screenshot: Joule – Example open orders (SAP S/4HANA)

Or you are a consultant who is new to the role, and a customer asks you how to customize a sales order in SAP S/4HANA. With Joule in SAP Mobile Start, you just pull out your phone and ask Joule, and you will get immediately the right SAP Help pages listed.

 Screenshot: Joule – Example documentation (SAP Help)

Finally, imagine you have a new team member, Maria, who has just joined from another team, and you want to quickly check when she has initially joined the company. Just ask Joule.

 
Screenshot: Joule – Example hiring date (SAP SuccessFactors)

Joule also allows you to navigate to other apps to get further details. Those could be web apps or native apps like the native SAP SuccessFactors app. So, if you want to learn more about Maria and her career so far, you just tap the “Profile” button and Joule will open Maria´s profile in the native SAP SuccessFactors app on your phone, with all the information about your new team member.

 Screenshot: Example native app integration

To initiate a Joule conversation, you're not limited to type in the request. You can now use Siri and Siri Shortcuts to seamlessly interact with Joule - which even works better with Apple Intelligence. Simply say "Hey Siri, ask SAP. Show my sales orders with delivery status not completed" and Joule will provide you with the respective content within the app.

Joule truly changes the way you interact with your business systems – it becomes much more natural. You can articulate your business needs in natural language and Joule responds by swiftly providing seamless access to your enablement content what will help you to execute your tasks faster and in a far more convenient way.

As of today, not all your prompts and web apps are supported on the phone; but more and more Joule business skills and scenarios are added. We are also working on adding some valuable mobile-specific Joule capabilities, so stay tuned for more.

If you have enabled Joule in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, or SAP Start, the mobile Joule client will automatically be available in SAP Mobile Start. No additional steps are needed.

Do not miss our brand new Joule in SAP Mobile Start video: “Bring Joule Everywhere! SAP's AI Copilot is Now Available on Mobile”. 

Besides bringing Joule to mobile, we have added more helpful features to further enhance your experience with SAP Mobile Start, like Personalization of the Start Screen. With the new release, you can easily move the Start Screen sections such as Insights or News up or down as they work best for you – and hide sections if you do not need them. As always, it is super easy and intuitive and will boost your productivity as you can adjust the Start Screen now, based on your preferences.

 Screenshot: SAP Start Screen personalization

Finally, we have enhanced the Apps and To-Dos Screen. Now you can expand and collapse your spaces and pages section on the app screen, and we have provided an enhanced filtering experience on the To-Do screen that allows you to set multiple filters.

Screenshot: To-Dos Screen with filtering option

I hope you will enjoy those new features in SAP Mobile Start.

For further information on the new topics, please check our SAP Mobile Start documentation.
SAP Mobile Experience offers intelligent native mobile solutions that help businesses build more efficient, resilient and sustainable end-to-end processes, improving people’s work life wherever they are.

Visit SAP Mobile Experience Community Page and click “follow” to get the latest development and innovation of our solutions. We look forward to hearing about your experience with setting up the solution in your landscape; please do share your thoughts and comments below. Enter here for additional questions regarding SAP Mobile Experience Applications.

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What' s New in Q4 2024
entry.content:
The Release Navigator for SAP BTP is always evolving to better serve our customers. With the latest update in Q4 2024, in addition to quarterly content maintenance we have added new product pages.

We collaborated closely with SAP BTP experts to add three new product pages that consolidate links to all available release communication channels, like schedules, pre- and post-release communication, blogs, and more.

The new pages are:

  1. SAP BTP, Kyma runtime
  2. SAP Mobile Services
  3. SAP Translation Hub

Read more about the details in this blog post.

 

1. SAP BTP, Kyma runtime

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, documentation on releases and available modules, the GitHub Kyma repositories, as well as a helpful learning journey and hands-on tutorials.

Check it out.

Fig 1. The new page for SAP BTP, Kyma runtime.

 

2. SAP Mobile Services

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, and helpful documentation.

Check it out.

Fig 2. The new page for SAP Mobile Services.

 

3. SAP Translation Hub

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, and helpful documentation.

Check it out.

Fig 3. The new page for SAP Translation Hub.

 

Currently, the release navigator for SAP BTP covers content for 30 products and services on dedicated pages. For all products and services not featured with their own page you can find helpful resources on the new General page.

Expect more new pages dedicated to specific products and services in future updates and don't hesitate to let us know via our feedback survey, which products or services you would like to see added next. 

 

Please note that the product page for SAP Data Intelligence Cloud has been retired as of this update. For more information, you can refer to this blog post.

 

For centralized information on when SAP BTP products and services release new features refer to the Consolidated Release Schedule for SAP BTP.

Regarding Release Navigator updates, you can refer to this update plan:

*Disclaimer:  The schedule is subject to change without prior notice including, when necessary, delays or incorporating changes in subsequent update windows and not a commitment, promise, or legal obligation to deliver any material, code, or functionality.

Looking ahead, 2025 updates will mainly focus on quarterly maintenance of release resources and a steady extension of product coverage. Currently, there are no plans for further mayor UI updates in the near future.

 

The Readiness@Scale team at SAP Enterprise Support is dedicated to ensuring release readiness across the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio. Our mission is to empower SAP customers to be prepared and ready to adopt and utilize innovations.

If you have any feedback or specific requests for content in the release navigator, please don't hesitate to reach out to us.

 

And don't forget to check out our team's blogs tagged with Readiness@Scale.

Happy navigating!

Readiness@Scale 
SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: Joule is Now Integrated into SAP Build Work Zone!
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to announce that Joule, SAP’s generative AI assistant, is now integrated into SAP Build Work Zone standard edition, SAP Start, and SAP Mobile Start. This marks a significant step in empowering users to work faster and smarter, with seamless access to insights and information at their fingertips.

Integrating Joule into SAP Build Work Zone brings a unique value to users by effortlessly connecting data from multiple backend systems into a single interface. By providing answers and insights from any supported system, Joule eliminates the need to switch between tools. This empowers users to stay informed with all relevant information and applications in one place.

What is Joule? 

Joule is SAP’s cutting-edge generative AI assistant, designed to bring intelligence and efficiency to every aspect of work. Joule helps users can interact naturally, ask questions, and get precise answers related to their business processes. For instance, end users can inquire about the status of a purchase order and be guided directly to it, streamlining their workflow and saving valuable time.

Joule isn’t just a productivity tool; it’s a game-changer for decision making and operational efficiency. It makes everyday tasks easier and more intuitive for end users.

 Very recently, Joule turned 1! Learn more about Joule here.

BIG NEWS: Joule in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

With our latest release, Joule is now integrated into SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. This enables end users to interact with Joule and get answers from all connected systems​.

This boosts productivity by leveraging Joule intuitive capabilities with SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. Users can navigate quickly to the functionality needed for their next task, receive precise answers on how to use SAP applications, and complete tasks effortlessly using natural language requests. It can help navigate to relevant applications and perform transactions. Additionally, Joule empowers more informed decision making with answers derived directly from your business document.

Joule can also serve as your personal guide within SAP Build Work Zone, helping you navigate and make the most of your site. Whether you need assistance with managing content or accessing specific features, Joule provides step-by-step guidance.

Here is an example of how you can use Joule within your Work Zone site: Imagine you're trying to track the status of a purchase order. Instead of navigating through multiple screens, you simply ask Joule, “Has my purchase order been approved?”. Joule instantly provides the status and key details directly within its window in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. This seamless interaction eliminates the need to switch between applications, saving time and effort.

Prerequisites for Using Joule in SAP Build Work Zone

To begin leveraging Joule in your SAP Build Work Zone environment, ensure the following prerequisites are met:

  • Joule requires Identity Authentication for user login. If you haven’t already switched to SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication, this step must be completed beforehand. For further guidance, see Switching to SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication.
  • Before completing the Joule onboarding tasks, it should be ensured that he required prerequisites for both SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and for Joule have been completed. 
  • Run the Joule booster to activate the integration and complete the setup process as seen in the steps below.

Set up subaccount by selecting the subaccount that should be used for Joule provisioning and configurations. 

Selecting the SAP solutions where Joule should be enabled.

Selecting the SAP solutions whose capability packages should be provisioned in Joule.

How to Get Started with the Integration

Integrating Joule into your SAP Build Work Zone is straightforward. After successfully running the Joule booster and completing the integration between SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule, a new setting becomes available in the Site Setting screen of your site.

 As an administrator, navigate to the Site Settings screen and enable the Joule option under the Services section. Once enabled, the Joule icon will appear in the header of your runtime site, giving users instant access to the AI-powered assistant for enhanced productivity and elevated outcomes across business.

Looking Ahead: What’s Next?

The integration of Joule into SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition is on the horizon and is coming in 2025. It will bring the Gen AI-powered experiences to an even broader set of users. The advanced edition will offer a wider scope with additional capabilities, tailored to meet diverse business needs. This expansion ensures that every end user can fully harness generative AI for greater efficiency and smarter decision-making.

Joule’s integration into SAP Build Work Zone is more than an enhancement—it's a transformation. It combines the power of SAP Build Work Zone with Joule's intelligence, letting users focus on what matters most. While the complexities of business processes are handled by a trusted AI assistant.

Stay tuned for latest updates as we bring even more innovation to your SAP Build experience.

***

Learn more:

➤ Announcement blog for Joule in SAP Mobile Start

➤ SAP Build Work Zone on: sap.com / SAP Community

➤ SAP Builders Community Group

➤ More information on new product features: Roadmap
